steps.title: Simplify the given equation
steps.content: Start with $13(x-n)=13y+13n$. Expanding gives $13x-13n=13y+13n$, so $13x-13y=26n$. Divide by $13$ to get $$x-y=2n.$$

steps.title: Use n = 1 as a Desmos check
steps.content: Since $n$ is positive, use $n=1$ to test the line behavior. The original equation becomes $x-y=2$, or $y=x-2$. Choice D becomes $2x-2y=2$, or $y=x-1$.

steps.title: Fit both lines and compare
steps.content: Make two small tables. The original line uses $(0,-2)$ and $(1,-1)$; choice D uses $(0,-1)$ and $(1,0)$. Regress each table. Desmos shows both slopes are $1$, but the intercepts are $-2$ and $-1$, so the lines are parallel and distinct.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is the same line as the original, so it gives infinitely many solutions. Choices B and C have $x+y$ after simplification, so they have slope $-1$ and cross the original. Choice D is parallel but separate, so there is no solution. Choice D is correct.
